title: Akshayapureeswarar Temple
tagline: Akshayapureeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Akshayapureeswarar Temple at Vilankulam (Vilangulam) in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu is a 13th-century Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva.
overview.description: The Akshayapureeswarar Temple at Vilankulam (Vilangulam) in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu is a 13th-century Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. The temple is known for its shrine of Shani, the graha god of the planet Saturn. It is one of the 27 Nakshatra (Natchathara) temples, associated with the 27 Nakshatra (lunar mansions). It is associated with Pushya (Poosam) nakshatra.

topPlaces.name: Senthalaivayal
topPlaces.description: Senthalaivayal is a village located on East Coast Road along the Bay of Bengal in Peravurani Taluk, Thanjavur District, Tamil Nadu, India. Senthalaivayal is a gram panchayat of Sethubhavachatram block people often called Senthalai Pattinam.

topPlaces.name: Perumagalur
topPlaces.description: Perumagalur is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Ambuliyar River
topPlaces.description: The Ambuliyar or Ambuli River is a non-perennial river in the Pudukkottai and Thanjavur districts of Tamil Nadu, India, that flows southeast into the Palk Strait.

topPlaces.name: Sethubavachatram
topPlaces.description: Sethubavachatram is a coastal village located in Peravurani taluk, Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India, with a population in 2011 of just over 2,000. It is largely a fishing and farming community.

items.note: Senthalaivayal is a village located on East Coast Road along the Bay of Bengal in Peravurani Taluk, Thanjavur District, Tamil Nadu, India. Senthalaivayal is a gram panchayat of Sethubhavachatram block people often called Senthalai Pattinam.
Perumagalur is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
The Ambuliyar or Ambuli River is a non-perennial river in the Pudukkottai and Thanjavur districts of Tamil Nadu, India, that flows southeast into the Palk Strait.
Sethubavachatram is a coastal village located in Peravurani taluk, Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India, with a population in 2011 of just over 2,000. It is largely a fishing and farming community.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Akshayapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Akshayapureeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Akshayapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (84 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (88 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Akshayapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Akshayapureeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Akshayapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Akshayapureeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Akshayapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Senthalaivayal, Perumagalur, Ambuliyar River, Sethubavachatram.
